Documentation","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-09-16T13:16:41Z","updated_at":"2024-09-18T01:39:02Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Issue with current documentation:

In the API reference for versions 2.0.0+, the parameter `mean` of the `numpy.var()` function is labeled to be new in 1.26.0. However, in the documentation for 1.26.0 this parameter is not mentioned, and it was not implemented in 1.26.4 (as far as I've seen). 

[https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.var.html](url) 

### Idea or request for content:

Change the version which it was added from 1.26.0 to @fjetter hope you're having a great day!

Couple of clarifying questions!
- How do I make the html docs when in the `dask-expr` repo? I don't see a makefile
- Should I just be adding three lines to generate the docstrings similar to `read_csv.__doc__ = READ_DOC_TEMPLATE.format(reader="read_csv", file_type="CSV")`
(for csv, table, and fwf) in dask-expr/dask-expr/io/csv.py)?
- Or should I be generating the doc string and copy and pasting the entire thing?

Apologies I'm relatively new to development! ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/dask/dask/issues/comments/2197899598/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
